name: Rust Game Engines
description: Game engines and game-development frameworks for Rust developers — full ECS engines, code-first frameworks, creative-coding toolkits, and bindings to non-Rust engines — compared across architecture, editor tooling, rendering, engine systems, platform reach, build-time cost, and ecosystem health

groups.name: General Information
groups.description: Basic identifying information, licensing, and maintenance status

attributes.name: Kind
attributes.description: Primary character of the tool; may list a secondary in a comment

attributes.name: License
attributes.description: Primary open-source license(s); note bindings whose underlying engine differs in a comment

attributes.name: Repository
attributes.description: Primary source repository (usually GitHub)

attributes.name: Crates.io
attributes.description: Main crates.io package page for the top-level crate

attributes.name: Documentation
attributes.description: Primary docs site — book, mdBook, or docs.rs

attributes.name: First Release
attributes.description: Year of first public release or first crates.io publish

attributes.name: Latest Stable Version
attributes.description: Current stable version from crates.io / releases page

attributes.name: Maintenance Status
attributes.description: Maintenance signals based on commit cadence over the last 6 months

attributes.name: Maturity
attributes.description: API stability, shipped games, and years since first release; most Rust engines are pre-1.0

attributes.name: Backing
attributes.description: Company, foundation, or funding model (or "Community" if none)

groups.name: Architecture & Programming Model
groups.description: Implementation, data model, scene authoring, and reload story

attributes.name: Implementation
attributes.description: Verify with cargo tree — bindings pull in *-sys crates or a bundled engine

attributes.name: Architecture
attributes.description: Select all that apply; note the dominant one in a comment

attributes.name: Built-in ECS
attributes.description: Ships a first-class ECS as the primary data model

attributes.name: Dimensionality
attributes.description: Select all supported with real first-party tooling (not just hand-rolled)

attributes.name: Scripting
attributes.description: What gameplay logic can be written in besides core Rust

attributes.name: Data-driven Scenes
attributes.description: Scenes/prefabs can be authored, serialized to files, and loaded at runtime — not only built in code

attributes.name: Reflection / Serialization
attributes.description: First-class runtime reflection or (de)serialization of game state

attributes.name: Hot Reload
attributes.description: Code or asset hot-reloading in dev; clarify scope in a comment

groups.name: Editor & Tooling
groups.description: Visual editor, inspector, asset pipeline, and dev tooling

attributes.name: Visual Editor
attributes.description: Ships a graphical scene/level editor

attributes.name: Live Inspector
attributes.description: Runtime entity/component/world inspector; note first-party vs de-facto in a comment

attributes.name: Asset Pipeline
attributes.description: 5=import/processing pipeline with reprocessing + hot reload; 3=basic async loading; 1=manual file loading only

attributes.name: CLI / Project Tooling
attributes.description: Project scaffolding and command-line tooling; select all

attributes.name: Debug / Profiling Tools
attributes.description: Debugging and profiling integrations; select all

attributes.name: IDE Support
attributes.description: Notable editor/IDE integrations beyond standard Rust tooling

groups.name: Rendering & Graphics
groups.description: Rendering backends, lighting, shaders, and 2D/3D graphics features

attributes.name: Rendering Backend
attributes.description: Supported rendering backends; select all

attributes.name: PBR
attributes.description: Physically-based rendering pipeline available for 3D (false for 2D-only tools)

attributes.name: Lighting
attributes.description: First-party lighting techniques supported; select all

attributes.name: Shadows
attributes.description: Real-time shadow mapping (3D) or 2D shadow support

attributes.name: Custom Shaders
attributes.description: User can author custom shaders (WGSL/GLSL/shader graph) and slot them into the pipeline

attributes.name: Sprites & Tilemaps
attributes.description: First-party or de-facto sprite batching and tilemap support; note plugin vs built-in in a comment

attributes.name: Particle System
attributes.description: Built-in or well-integrated particle/VFX system

attributes.name: Post-processing
attributes.description: Post-processing effects (bloom, tonemapping, FXAA/TAA) available

attributes.name: Text Rendering
attributes.description: Built-in font/text rendering (glyph atlas, layout)

groups.name: Engine Systems
groups.description: Physics, audio, input, animation, UI, networking, and model import

attributes.name: Physics
attributes.description: How physics is provided — bundled, official plugin, or bring-your-own

attributes.name: Audio
attributes.description: How audio is provided — bundled, official plugin, or bring-your-own

attributes.name: Input
attributes.description: First-party input support; select all

attributes.name: Animation
attributes.description: Animation techniques supported; select all

attributes.name: UI System
attributes.description: In-game/tooling UI approach

attributes.name: Networking
attributes.description: Multiplayer/replication support; name the de-facto plugin in a comment

attributes.name: glTF / Model Import
attributes.description: Loads standard 3D model formats (glTF/GLB); false for 2D-only tools

groups.name: Platform Support
groups.description: Officially supported and tested deployment targets

attributes.name: Windows
attributes.description: Native Windows target

attributes.name: macOS
attributes.description: Native macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel)

attributes.name: Linux
attributes.description: Native Linux (X11 and/or Wayland); note if only one in a comment

attributes.name: Web (WASM)
attributes.description: Compiles to WebAssembly and runs in a browser

attributes.name: iOS
attributes.description: Ships to iOS; only true if officially supported/tested

attributes.name: Android
attributes.description: Ships to Android

attributes.name: Consoles
attributes.description: Only mark a console with a verifiable path; explain any unofficial routes in a comment

groups.name: Performance & Footprint
groups.description: Build times, binary and bundle sizes, and runtime overhead

attributes.name: Clean Build Time
attributes.description: Cold release build of a minimal "hello window" project from a clean target dir; record host CPU + date in a comment

attributes.name: Incremental Build Time
attributes.description: Rebuild after a one-line gameplay change; note dynamic linking / mold / cranelift use

attributes.name: Binary Size
attributes.description: Stripped release build of a minimal app

attributes.name: Web Bundle Size
attributes.description: WASM binary of a minimal app (post-wasm-opt); only for web-capable tools

attributes.name: Runtime Overhead
attributes.description: Subjective idle CPU/memory of a trivial app; note methodology in a comment, prefer null over a guess

groups.name: Ecosystem & Community
groups.description: Adoption signals, shipped titles, community size, and learning resources

attributes.name: GitHub Stars
attributes.description: Snapshot star count; record the date in a comment

attributes.name: Crates.io Downloads
attributes.description: All-time downloads of the top-level crate; null for editor-first engines not on crates.io

attributes.name: Last Release
attributes.description: Date of the most recent tagged/crates.io release

attributes.name: Shipped Titles
attributes.description: Notable released games known to use this engine; avoid listing jam prototypes

attributes.name: Community Size
attributes.description: Approximate community scale (Discord/forum members, contributor count)

attributes.name: Plugin / Asset Ecosystem
attributes.description: Availability of third-party plugins/crates

attributes.name: Learning Resources
attributes.description: 5=official book + tutorials + videos + examples; 3=decent examples + some guides; 1=README + sparse examples only
